content:
# Numbers Chapter 36 — The LOGOS Bible (Formal)
**Translator:** LOGOS | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pp. 194-195 | **Source Image:** `NUM_036_page_001.jpg`, `NUM_036_page_002.jpg`
**Secondary Source:** Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), p. pa_0142

---

**1** And the heads of the fathers of the clan of the sons of Gilead, the son of Machir, the son of Manasseh, from the clans of the sons of Joseph, drew near and spoke before Moses and before the leaders, the heads of the fathers of the sons of Israel.

**2** And they said, "YHWH commanded my lord to give the land as an inheritance by lot to the sons of Israel, and my lord was commanded by YHWH to give the inheritance of Zelophehad our brother to his daughters.

**3** And if they become wives to any of the sons of the other tribes of the sons of Israel, then their inheritance will be taken away from the inheritance of our fathers and will be added to the inheritance of the tribe to which they belong; and it will be taken away from the lot of our inheritance.

**4** And when the Jubilee of the sons of Israel comes, then their inheritance will be added to the inheritance of the tribe to which they belong, and their inheritance will be taken away from the inheritance of the tribe of our fathers."

**5** And Moses commanded the sons of Israel according to the word of YHWH, saying, "The tribe of the sons of Joseph speaks rightly.

**6** This is the thing that YHWH has commanded concerning the daughters of Zelophehad, saying: Let them become wives to whomever is good in their eyes, only they shall become wives within the clan of the tribe of their father.

**7** And the inheritance of the sons of Israel shall not turn about from tribe to tribe, for each one of the sons of Israel shall cling to the inheritance of the tribe of his fathers.

**8** And every daughter who possesses an inheritance from the tribes of the sons of Israel shall become a wife to one from the clan of the tribe of her father, so that each one of the sons of Israel may possess the inheritance of his fathers.

**9** And no inheritance shall turn about from one tribe to another tribe, for each one of the tribes of the sons of Israel shall cling to its own inheritance."

**10** As YHWH commanded Moses, so the daughters of Zelophehad did.

**11** And Mahlah, Tirzah, and Hoglah, and Milcah, and Noah, the daughters of Zelophehad, became wives to the sons of their father's brothers.

**12** They became wives into the clans of the sons of Manasseh, the son of Joseph, and their inheritance remained with the tribe of the clan of their father.

**13** These are the commandments and the ordinances that YHWH commanded by the hand of Moses to the sons of Israel in the plains of Moab, by the Jordan at Jericho.

---

## LOGOS Translation Notes

### Verse 1 — מִשְׁפְּחַת (mishpachat)
The Hebrew מִשְׁפְּחַת is rendered "clan" to distinguish it from מַטֶּה (matteh, "tribe") and בֵּית אָבוֹת (beit avot, "fathers' house"). The genealogical chain — Gilead son of Machir son of Manasseh — connects this petition back to the broader Josephite inheritance. The Vaticanus reads δήμου (demou, "clan/people"), confirming the sub-tribal unit.

### Verse 2 — אֲדֹנִי (adoni)
"My lord" — a respectful address to Moses as judicial authority, not a divine title. The petitioners invoke YHWH's prior command (Numbers 27:7) to establish legal precedent for their complaint.

### Verses 3-4 — יוֹבֵל (yovel)
The "Jubilee" (יוֹבֵל) is the fiftieth-year land restoration prescribed in Leviticus 25. The petitioners argue that Jubilee provisions, which normally return land to its original tribal allotment, would not remedy this case because the inheritance would have legitimately transferred through marriage, not through sale.

### Verse 5 — כֵּן (ken)
"Rightly" — literally "right" or "correct." Moses affirms the legal validity of the Gileadite petition. The LXX Vaticanus uses ὀρθῶς (orthos), confirming this sense of juridical correctness.

### Verse 6 — לַטּוֹב בְּעֵינֵיהֶם (lattov be'einehem)
"To whomever is good in their eyes" — a formal rendering of the Hebrew idiom preserving the daughters' freedom of choice within the prescribed boundary. The constraint אַךְ (akh, "only") introduces the sole limitation: endogamy within the father's tribal clan.

### Verse 7 — תִסֹּב (tissov)
"Shall not turn about" — from the root סָבַב (savav, "to turn, go around"). This verb appears repeatedly in this chapter (vv. 7, 9) to describe the prohibited circular transfer of tribal inheritance. The Vaticanus uses περιστραφήσεται (peristraphesetai), preserving the same rotational metaphor.

### Verse 8 — יֹרֶשֶׁת (yoreshet)
"Who possesses" — feminine participle of יָרַשׁ (yarash, "to possess, inherit"). This verse extends the ruling beyond Zelophehad's daughters to a universal statute applying to all daughter-heiresses throughout Israel.

### Verse 11 — Order of Daughters' Names
The order here — Mahlah, Tirzah, Hoglah, Milcah, Noah — differs from the order in Numbers 27:1 (Mahlah, Noah, Hoglah, Milcah, Tirzah). The Leningrad Codex preserves this variant ordering. Some scholars suggest the differing orders reflect birth order versus another arrangement (perhaps marriage order). The Vaticanus follows the same order as the MT here.

### Verse 12 — לְמִשְׁפְּחֹת (lemishpechot)
"Into the clans" — the preposition לְ with the plural of מִשְׁפָּחָה indicates marriage into (not merely alongside) the Manassite clans, reinforcing that the inheritance remained within the tribal boundary.

### Verse 13 — Colophon of Numbers
This verse serves as the closing colophon for the entire book of Numbers, paralleling similar formulae at the end of Leviticus (Lev 27:34). The phrase בְּעַרְבֹת מוֹאָב (be'arvot Mo'av, "in the plains of Moab") locates the final legislation in the Transjordanian camp. עַל־יַרְדֵּן יְרֵחוֹ ("by the Jordan at Jericho") further specifies the location as opposite Jericho, on the threshold of the promised land — a fitting conclusion to the book that narrates Israel's wilderness journey from Sinai to the edge of Canaan.

### Textual Note — MT and LXX Agreement
The Vaticanus LXX text of Numbers 36 is in substantial agreement with the Masoretic Text of the Leningrad Codex. No significant divergences affect the translation. Minor differences include the LXX's slightly more explicit phrasing in verse 2 and standard Greek syntactical rearrangements, but no variant readings require comment in the translation proper.
